title: Summer Dawn Bird Walk - RVU Campus
shortDescription: A guided sunrise campus trail to observe resident and migratory birds before classes begin.
fullDescription: Join TGA's early-morning birdwatching walk across key green pockets of the RVU campus. We will document species sightings, discuss behavior patterns, and log observations that support our biodiversity assessment project.

title: Great Backyard Bird Count 2025 - RVU Participation
location: RV University and nearby Kengeri birding patches
shortDescription: TGA's annual participation in GBBC with checklist submissions to global citizen science databases.
fullDescription: During GBBC 2025, TGA members split into teams and covered the RVU campus and adjacent habitats to document bird diversity. The event combined beginner orientation, checklist quality checks, and collaborative data submission on eBird.

title: Kengeri Lake Cleanup Drive
shortDescription: Hands-on cleanup in collaboration with student volunteers and local community partners.
fullDescription: TGA's cleanup drive focused on litter removal, waste segregation, and awareness outreach near Kengeri Lake. Volunteers documented plastic hotspots and shared findings with local stakeholders for follow-up action.

title: Expert Talk: Urban Wildlife Corridors in Bengaluru
shortDescription: An expert session by NCF on habitat connectivity and practical urban conservation pathways.
fullDescription: This invited talk by a Nature Conservation Foundation expert explores how wildlife corridors function in cities like Bengaluru. The session covers case studies, landscape pressures, and student-friendly ways to support habitat continuity.

title: World Migratory Bird Day Walk
location: RVU Campus Trail + Nearby Open Wet Patch
shortDescription: A themed bird walk focusing on migratory routes, stopover habitats, and conservation messaging.
fullDescription: In observance of World Migratory Bird Day, TGA hosts a focused walk to identify migratory species and discuss threats along flyways. Participants will contribute sightings to shared records and receive a brief orientation on ethical field behavior.

title: Biodiversity Assessment Field Day - Open to Volunteers
location: RV University Campus Sectors A, C, and E
shortDescription: A practical field session introducing sampling methods and digital logging for campus biodiversity data.
fullDescription: This volunteer-friendly field day trains participants on transect walks, species note-taking, and iNaturalist logging basics. The data collected contributes directly to TGA's ongoing campus biodiversity assessment and future conservation planning.
